title: Senior Staff Specialist, External Capacity & Supplier Alignment
description:
our new responsibilities

  • Own the process for sharing the consolidated 18-month rolling demand forecast with designated key suppliers (Foundries, OSATs) to initiate capacity planning cycles.
  • Receive, analyze, and challenge supplier capacity commitments, ensuring alignment with forecast and internal inventory/service level goals.
  • Drive and facilitate dedicated meetings (e.g., Forecast Review, Capacity Gap Closure) with internal and external stakeholders to understand demand/forecast changes, define clear capacity gap closure action plans, and secure necessary supply.
  • Model and maintain confirmed external supplier capacity data in the Advanced Planning System (APS) as basis for the internal supply planning and Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) cycle.
  • Proactively identify potential capacity risks, trade-offs, and supply bottlenecks at the external supplier level, and escalate critical issues to S&OP, Supplier Planning, Operations and Procurement leadership.
  • Support the strategic supplier relationship by coordinating business meetings and preparing materials for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) for key external partners.
  • Set-up and monitor key supplier performance indicators (KPIs) related to capacity commitment accuracy and adherence.
  • Coordinate and prepare the monthly external S&OP report-out detailing supplier capacity performance, risks, and proposed action items for Management Team (MT) review.
  • Govern and standardize modeling of external capacities, driving requirements for advanced planning system enhancements.
  • Integrate Product Lifecycle Management (NPI/EOL) into the capacity planning process to proactively manage demand transitions


What we look for

  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in supply chain management, Industrial Engineering, or a related technical field.
  • Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in S&OP, Demand Planning, or Supplier/ External Capacity Management
  • Essential experience within the semiconductor manufacturing environment (Foundry or OSAT exposure is highly preferred).
  • Proven experience in managing complex, long-lead-time supplier relationships in a high-volume, high-mix setting.
  • Strong hands-on experience in an end-to-end Supply Chain, Logistics, or Factory Planning function.
  • Advanced Planning Systems (APS): Working proficiency in Advanced Planning Systems (e.g., Kinaxis, SAP IBP, Blue Yonder) specifically related to capacity modeling and supplier interface modules.
  • Data & Analytics: Proficiency in Data Analytics and Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Power BI) for monitoring supplier performance and analyzing large datasets.
  • ERP Systems: Solid working knowledge of ERP system logic (e.g., SAP) related to Purchase Orders and master data impacting external supply.

postedCompany.description: The ams OSRAM Group is a global leader in optical solutions. By adding intelligence to light and passion to innovation, we enrich peoples' lives. This is what we mean by Sensing is Life. Our around 26,000 employees worldwide focus on innovation across sensing, illumination and visualization to make journeys safer, medical diagnoses more accurate and daily moments in communication a richer experience.
